items.title: Chinese APT deploys new malware to keep access to hacked networks
items.summary: A Chinese espionage group tracked as UNC5221 has been accessing Microsoft 365 environments using the Brickstorm backdoor and previously undocumented malware named Plenet and AgentPSD. [...]

items.title: Dark web Nemesis Market vendor gets 26 years for selling drugs
items.summary: A California man was sentenced to more than 26 years in federal prison for trafficking fentanyl and methamphetamine through Nemesis Market, one of the world's largest dark web marketplaces. [...]

items.title: The most interesting startups right now want to get you off your phone
items.summary: While the AI fundraising machine keeps breaking its own records, some founders are building in the other direction.  Mirror founder Brynn Putnam just raised money for Board, a startup focused on bringing people together through in-person games and social experiences. Cyberdeck creators are going viral crafting whimsical DIY computers that literally encourage users to touch grass. Unlike the AI-free browser crowd, this doesn’t just feel like backlash, […]

items.title: Building secure B2C applications with fine-grained access control using Amazon Cognito and Amazon Verified Permissions
items.summary: Modern web applications require robust security controls to protect user data and application resources. Authentication and authorization are two fundamental pillars of application security that answer critical questions: Who are you? and What are you allowed to do? Implementing these controls correctly can be challenging for developers, especially when building data-intensive applications with frameworks like […]

items.title: Connecting Vulnerability Intelligence to Real-World Exposure With Flashpoint EASM
items.summary:
With the introduction of EASM in Flashpoint Ignite, organizations can continuously discover internet-facing assets, map them to Flashpoint Vulnerability Intelligence, and prioritize remediation efforts based on actual risk rather than vulnerability volume and severity alone.
The post Connecting Vulnerability Intelligence to Real-World Exposure With Flashpoint EASM appeared first on Flashpoint.

items.title: OWASP Incubator Project Helps Developers Find and Fix Vulnerable Dependencies in Seconds
items.summary:
CVE Lite CLI is a free, open-source command line tool that scans your projects in seconds and tells you exactly which included packages contain a vulnerability.
The post OWASP Incubator Project Helps Developers Find and Fix Vulnerable Dependencies in Seconds appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: New York lawmakers pass one-year ban on new data centers
items.summary: The New York State legislature passed a one-year moratorium on new large data centers, the first statewide ban of its kind if Democratic Governor Kathy Hochul signs it into law. Lawmakers behind the bill say it's meant to give policymakers time to understand the impact of large data centers on the environment and energy prices. […]

items.title: Android Spyware Asin Targets Arabic Users via Fake News, PDF and War Map Apps
items.summary:
Arabic-speaking users have emerged as the target of a new Android spyware codenamed Asin, according to findings from ESET.

The Slovakian cybersecurity company said it first detected the malware spread via multiple campaigns in early 2025, with each attack wave making use of distinct websites mimicking utilities, war-related updates, and a government news source:

items.title: Over 900 US gas station tank gauge systems exposed to attacks
items.summary: Over 900 automatic tank gauge (ATG) systems across the United States, used to monitor fuel and chemical storage tanks across various critical infrastructure sectors, have been found exposed online and are vulnerable to ongoing attacks. [...]

items.title: EU unveils tech sovereignty package to cut reliance on US, Chinese suppliers
items.summary: The package bundles two draft laws — a Chips Act 2.0 and a Cloud and AI Development Act (CADA) — alongside an Open Source Strategy and a roadmap for digitalizing the energy system.

items.title: New Threat Cluster OP-512 Targets Microsoft IIS Servers with Custom Web Shell Framework
items.summary:
Cybersecurity researchers have discovered a previously unreported threat cluster dubbed OP-512 (where "OP" stands for "opponent") that has been observed targeting Microsoft Internet Information Services (IIS) servers to deploy a bespoke web shell framework.

ReliaQuest has assessed with moderate to high confidence that the espionage-focused activity is linked to China.

"OP-512 was highly

items.title: CISA Adds One Known Exploited Vulnerability to Catalog
items.summary:
CISA has added one new vulnerability to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) Catalog, based on evidence of active exploitation.

CVE-2026-28318 SolarWinds Serv-U Uncontrolled Resource Consumption Vulnerability

This type of vulnerability is a frequent attack vector for malicious cyber actors and poses significant risks to the federal enterprise.
Binding Operational Directive (BOD) 22-01: Reducing the Significant Risk of Known Exploited Vulnerabilities established the KEV Catalog as a living list of known Common Vulnerabilities and Exposures (CVEs) that carry significant risk to the federa

items.summary: Eighteen months ago, the AI SOC was a marketing line. Today it's a budget item. The category has crossed over from interesting to inevitable, with billions of dollars now flowing into AI-powered security operations platforms, agentic SOC tools, and AI co-pilots built into every layer of the security stack. The data shows SOCs are buying, deploying, and standing up AI capabilities at the fastest

items.title: Chrome 149 Patches 429 Vulnerabilities
items.summary:
Over 100 bugs are critical or high-severity, mainly use-after-free and insufficient validation of untrusted input flaws.
The post Chrome 149 Patches 429 Vulnerabilities appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Quoting Andreas Kling
items.summary:
We will no longer accept public pull requests. [...]
A substantial patch used to imply substantial effort, and that effort was a reasonable proxy for good faith. That assumption no longer holds. [...]
Whether code was typed by hand is beside the point. What matters is who is responsible for it once it enters the browser. Ladybird is becoming a browser for real users. The people introducing changes to it must be the people who decide those changes belong in the project, and who will answer for the consequences.
— Andreas Kling, Changing How We Develop Ladybird

    Tags: ladybird, ai-ethi

items.title: Hackers Exploit Critical Everest Forms Pro WordPress Plugin Flaw to Take Over Sites
items.summary:
Threat actors are actively exploiting a critical security flaw in Everest Forms Pro, a WordPress plugin with about 4,000 active installations, to execute arbitrary code, leading to a complete site compromise.

The vulnerability in question is CVE-2026-3300 (CVSS score: 9.8), a remote code execution bug impacting all versions of the plugin up to, and including, 1.9.12. A patch for the flaw was

items.summary:

items.title: FIFA World Cup 2026 Scams Are Already Live: Fake Sites, Banking Malware, and Stolen Logins
items.summary:
Security researchers and the FBI are warning that a wave of FIFA-themed fraud is already hitting World Cup 2026 fans, days before the June 11 kickoff.

Recent reports describe thousands of lookalike FIFA domains, banking malware hidden inside pirate streaming apps, and at least one operation that copies FIFA's login page well enough to take over real accounts.

items.summary: On Thursday, Cisco warned of a high-severity, unpatched zero-day in the Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Manager (tracked as CVE-2026-20245) actively exploited in attacks enabling root privilege escalation. [...]

items.title: Cisco Warns of 7th SD-WAN Zero-Day Exploited in 2026
items.summary:

items.title: PCPJack Hijacks 230 AWS, Google Cloud, and Azure Servers for Covert SMTP Relay Network
items.summary:
The threat actor known as PCPJack has hijacked cloud servers associated with Amazon Web Services (AWS), Google Cloud, and Microsoft Azure to create a covert SMTP email relay network.

"Compromised business servers across the U.S., Europe, and Asia were quietly converted into SMTP proxies, verified for mail relay capability, and synced to a downstream consumer every five minutes," Hunt.io said in

items.title: Mira Murati steps back into the spotlight, carefully
items.summary: In the current environment, remaining heads down has diminishing returns; at some point, you have to make some noise just to remind the market you exist.
